transcript: Every project passes through distinct phases called the project life cycle. The life cycle provides a basic framework for managing the project regardless of the specific work involved. A typical project life cycle consists of four phases: starting the project, organizing and preparing, carrying out the work, and closing the project. During the starting phase project managers identify the business need and ensure that the project is feasible. In the organizing and preparing phase the team creates a detailed project management plan. The carrying out phase involves executing the plan and monitoring progress. Finally the closing phase formalizes acceptance of the product and brings the project to an orderly end.
